title: Itchy scalp in athletes: 10 teas to try

remedies.remedy_desc: Melaleuca alternifolia teas, also known as tea tree oil tea, may help with an itchy scalp due to its antifungal and antibacterial properties. The main constituents, including cineole, terpinen-4-ol, and limonene, have been shown to reduce inflammation and kill fungi that can cause itchiness.  To try using Melaleuca alternifolia tea for an itchy scalp, start by brewing a cup of tea using 1 teaspoon of dried tea tree leaves in 8 ounces of boiling water. Let it steep for 5-7 minutes and then strain it. You can apply this tea directly to your scalp using a cotton ball, or add it to your regular shampoo.  Applying the tea to your scalp may help reduce itchiness and inflammation, and may also help to soothe and calm irritated skin. However, it's essential to note that individual results may vary, and it's best to do a patch test before applying the tea to a larger area.
remedies.image_alt: Melaleuca alternifolia herbal teas for itchy scalp

remedies.remedy_desc: Rosmarinus officinalis teas, also known as rosemary tea, may help with an itchy scalp due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. Rosemary tea contains compounds like carnosic acid and rosmarinic acid, which have been shown to reduce inflammation and fight off bacteria and fungi that can cause irritation. The antiseptic properties of rosemary tea can also help to soothe and calm the scalp, reducing redness and itching. To try rosemary tea for an itchy scalp, start by making a strong tea by steeping 1 teaspoon of dried rosemary leaves in 8 ounces of boiling water for 5-7 minutes. Strain the tea and let it cool before applying it to the scalp. You can use a cotton ball or soft cloth to apply the tea to the affected area, or add it to your regular shampoo or conditioner. When you use rosemary tea for an itchy scalp, you may experience a reduction in inflammation and itching, and an improvement in scalp health. The tea may also help to promote hair growth and reduce dandruff. However, it's essential to note that rosemary tea may not work for everyone, and it's always a good idea to do a patch test before using it on a larger area.
remedies.image_alt: Rosmarinus officinalis herbal teas for itchy scalp

remedies.remedy_desc: Urtica dioica teas, also known as nettle tea, can help alleviate an itchy scalp due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The tea contains compounds like flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which work together to soothe and calm the skin on your scalp. These constituents help to reduce inflammation and kill bacteria that can cause itchiness and irritation. To use Urtica dioica tea for an itchy scalp, you can start by brewing a cup of the tea and letting it cool. Then, apply the cooled tea directly to your scalp using a cotton ball or a gentle massage. Leave the tea on for about 15-20 minutes before rinsing it off with warm water. You can repeat this process a few times a week to see improvement. After using Urtica dioica tea, you may notice a decrease in itchiness and redness on your scalp, and your skin may feel softer and more balanced. Additionally, the tea's antifungal properties may help to prevent fungal infections that can contribute to an itchy scalp.
remedies.image_alt: Urtica dioica herbal teas for itchy scalp

remedies.remedy_desc: Cymbopogon citratus teas, also known as lemongrass tea, has been used for centuries to soothe an itchy scalp. The tea contains compounds like citral and geraniol, which have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties that help reduce redness and irritation. The tea's antifungal properties also combat fungal infections that can cause itchiness and flakiness. To use lemongrass tea for an itchy scalp, start by brewing a cup of tea by steeping a tablespoon of dried lemongrass leaves in boiling water for 5-7 minutes. Strain the tea and apply it directly to the scalp as a final rinse after shampooing. You can also drink the tea internally to help soothe from the inside out.  When you apply lemongrass tea to your scalp, you can expect the itchiness to reduce within a few minutes. The anti-inflammatory properties will help calm the skin, while the antiseptic properties will prevent further infections. Regular use of lemongrass tea may also help promote healthy hair growth by reducing dandruff and other scalp conditions. If your scalp is severely itchy or irritated, it's best to do a patch test before using lemongrass tea extensively. This will ensure that you don't have any adverse reactions to the tea.
remedies.image_alt: Cymbopogon citratus herbal teas for itchy scalp

remedies.remedy_desc: Aloe vera teas can be a helpful remedy for an itchy scalp due to its anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. Aloe vera contains compounds like aloin and aloe-emodin, which have been shown to reduce inflammation and irritation on the skin. The gel extracted from the plant also contains vitamins A, C, and E, as well as minerals like calcium and potassium, which can help calm and moisturize the scalp.  To try aloe vera tea for an itchy scalp, start by making a tea by steeping 2 tablespoons of aloe vera gel in a cup of hot water for 5-7 minutes. Strain the liquid and let it cool before applying it to your scalp using a cotton ball or a soft brush. You can also mix aloe vera gel with coconut oil or olive oil and apply it to your scalp before shampooing.  When you apply aloe vera tea to your itchy scalp, you may experience a reduction in itching and inflammation within a few days. Your scalp may feel cooler and more comfortable, and you may notice a decrease in flaking or dandruff. Repeated use of aloe vera tea may lead to long-term relief from itchy scalp symptoms, and some people have reported improvements in their scalp health after regular use.
remedies.image_alt: Aloe vera herbal teas for itchy scalp

remedies.remedy_desc: Aloe barbadensis teas have been used for centuries to soothe an itchy scalp due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The tea contains compounds like aloin, aloe-emodin, and vitamin E, which work together to calm irritated skin and reduce redness. When you drink aloe barbadensis tea, the vitamins and minerals are absorbed into the bloodstream, helping to reduce inflammation and promote healthy scalp conditions.  To use aloe barbadensis tea for an itchy scalp, you can try the following: first, steep a teaspoon of dried aloe barbadensis leaves in a cup of boiling water for 5-7 minutes; let it cool down before applying it to your scalp as a rinse or a hair mask. Alternatively, you can drink 1-2 cups of the tea per day to reap its benefits internally.  As you start to use aloe barbadensis tea, you may notice a decrease in itchiness and redness on your scalp within a few days. Your scalp may also start to feel calmer and more balanced, allowing your hair to grow healthier and stronger. However, results may vary depending on the severity of your condition, so it's essential to be patient and consistent with your treatment.
remedies.image_alt: Aloe barbadensis herbal teas for itchy scalp

remedies.remedy_desc: Thymus vulgaris teas, also known as thyme tea, have been used for centuries to soothe an itchy scalp. The active compounds in thyme, such as thymol and carvacrol, have antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties that help to calm the skin and reduce irritation. When applied topically or consumed as a tea, these compounds can help to combat fungal infections, such as ringworm, that can cause itching and flaking on the scalp. To try thyme tea for an itchy scalp, brew a cup of thyme leaves in hot water, then let it cool to room temperature. Strain the tea and apply it to your scalp using a cotton ball or swab, or drink it as a tea. As the thymol and carvacrol in thyme tea work to reduce inflammation and kill off fungal infections, you may start to notice a reduction in itching and flaking within a few days. Regular use of thyme tea may also help to prevent future outbreaks and promote a healthy scalp.
remedies.image_alt: Thymus vulgaris herbal teas for itchy scalp

remedies.remedy_desc: Saponaria officinalis teas have been used to help with itchy scalp due to its anti-inflammatory and antifungal properties. The tea is rich in saponins, which are natural compounds that have been shown to soothe and calm irritated skin. These saponins help to reduce redness and inflammation, making it an effective remedy for itchy scalp. Additionally, the tea has antibacterial properties, which help to control the growth of fungi and bacteria that can cause itchiness. To try Saponaria officinalis teas for an itchy scalp, start by making a tea by steeping 1 teaspoon of dried Saponaria officinalis flowers in 1 cup of hot water for 5-7 minutes. Let it cool, then use it as a final rinse after shampooing your hair. You can also add a few drops of the tea to your regular shampoo for extra benefits. It's essential to note that you may need to try this remedy for a few days to see noticeable results. When you start using Saponaria officinalis teas, you may notice a decrease in itchiness and inflammation. Your scalp may feel soothed and calmer, and you may even see a reduction in flaking or dandruff. As the tea works to control fungal and bacterial growth, you may also notice a reduction in the severity of your itchy scalp. However, it's essential to be patient and consistent with your treatment, as it may take some time to see the full effects.
remedies.image_alt: Saponaria officinalis herbal teas for itchy scalp

remedies.remedy_desc: Eucalyptus globulus teas have been used for centuries to soothe an itchy scalp. The leaves of the Eucalyptus tree contain compounds like eucalyptol, a natural decongestant and anti-inflammatory, and tannins, which help reduce irritation. These properties help calm the scalp, reducing redness and itching. The tea's antifungal and antibacterial properties also combat scalp infections that can cause itchiness. To use Eucalyptus globulus teas for an itchy scalp, start by making a tea by steeping a teaspoon of dried Eucalyptus leaves in a cup of boiling water for 5-7 minutes. Strain the tea and let it cool. You can use it as a final rinse after shampooing or apply it directly to your scalp with a cotton ball. Leave it on for 15-20 minutes before rinsing with warm water. Repeat this process a few times a week to see improvement. By using Eucalyptus globulus teas, you may notice a reduction in scalp itchiness and inflammation within a few days. The tea's antiseptic properties can help heal scalp infections, while its soothing effects can calm redness and irritation. As you continue to use the tea, you may find that your scalp feels cooler and less itchy, and that your hair looks healthier and shinier. However, if your symptoms persist or worsen, it's best to consult a dermatologist for further advice.
remedies.image_alt: Eucalyptus globulus herbal teas for itchy scalp

remedies.remedy_desc: Artemisia absinthium teas have been used for centuries to soothe an itchy scalp, and it's not hard to see why. The tea is rich in antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, thanks to its high concentration of compounds like thujone, camphor, and borneol. These properties help to reduce redness, calm irritation, and even kill off fungal and bacterial infections that can cause itchiness. The tea also has antipruritic effects, which means it directly helps to reduce itching sensations. To try Artemisia absinthium tea for an itchy scalp, start by steeping one teaspoon of dried wormwood leaves in a cup of boiling water for five to seven minutes. Strain the tea and let it cool before applying it to your scalp as a compress or using it as a final rinse after shampooing. You can also add a few drops of the tea to your shampoo or conditioner for an extra dose of relief. It's essential to note that wormwood tea can be quite potent, so start with small amounts and gradually increase as needed. When you use Artemisia absinthium tea for an itchy scalp, you can expect to see some relief from itching and inflammation within a few days. The tea may help to clear up fungal infections like ringworm, and reduce redness and irritation caused by allergies or skin conditions like eczema. However, it's crucial to remember that wormwood tea can interact with certain medications and worsen some health conditions, so always consult with a healthcare professional before using it, especially if you're pregnant, breastfeeding, or taking medications.
remedies.image_alt: Artemisia absinthium herbal teas for itchy scalp

intro_desc: Athletes often experience an itchy scalp due to tight headgear, sweaty helmets, and frequent washing of hair with harsh chemicals. This constant irritation can lead to discomfort, distraction, and even affect their performance. Imagine trying to focus on a game or a workout while constantly feeling like scratching your scalp. It can be frustrating, especially when you're trying to give your best. The itchiness can also cause anxiety, making it harder to sleep and recover from physical activities. It's not just about the itch, it's about how it affects their daily lives and overall well-being.  When athletes experience an itchy scalp, they may try various treatments, but sometimes these treatments can be harsh or have side effects. That's where herbal teas come in. These teas are a natural and gentle way to soothe the scalp and reduce itching. They work by calming the skin, reducing inflammation, and promoting a healthy scalp environment. By incorporating herbal teas into their routine, athletes can find relief from itchy scalp and focus on what matters most – their performance and recovery.
